campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the city's business license tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the proposed changes to the municipal code, and the council discussed and deliberated on the new rates before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased
Changes take effect in the new fiscal year
Finance Department led the presentation and analysis
City Council debated and voted on the new rates

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents could voice their opinions on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (city law) and decided to fast-track it by skipping the normal first reading process, with plans to formally adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, review the town's finances for February, and authorize the Town Manager to sign contracts for construction services and professional agreements. The council also moved forward with approving improvements to town infrastructure.
keyTopics: Approved financial records and February 2026 spending report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction services
Approved a three-year professional services agreement
Approved town infrastructure improvement plans

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026 to organize their upcoming agendas. They reviewed and finalized items for the May 5th meeting, then looked ahead to plan the May 12th meeting, with options to add new topics and set specific times for certain discussions.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Planned new items for the May 12, 2026 draft agenda
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026
Set time slots for specific agenda items as needed
Addressed any required document waiver notices

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to review a draft plan for improving biodiversity and urban forests in the city. The council also recognized the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center for winning a state award and proclaimed April as Fair Housing Month to promote equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan draft review
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center receives California Parks and Recreation Society Award of Excellence
Fair Housing Month Proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to attend in person or watch online and provide public comment during the meeting.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online meeting participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents could speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The committee approved minutes from their previous January meeting and reviewed how well the city's pension and retiree health benefit investments performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on city matters
Approval of January 26, 2026 meeting minutes
Pension and retiree health benefit fund performance (Q1 2026)
Investment returns on city's Section 115 Trust accounts

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union for 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to review and approve several important financial and development plans for the upcoming year. The council discussed tax policies, development fees, user fees, and heard a presentation about the county's plan to address homelessness in the community.
keyTopics: Approval of tax and fiscal responsibility policies
FY 2026-27 development fees and regulations
Updates to user fees for city services
County homelessness prevention strategy
Approval of meeting minutes and future meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several community projects and programs. Key items included updates on improving the Foothills Nature Preserve, plans for how to run the middle school athletics program going forward, and approval of the Parks and Recreation department's work plan for next year.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project
Middle School Athletics Program management and future direction
Parks and Recreation Commission work plan for 2027
Department updates and liaison reports
